poem.content:
A shallow case, a mobile screen’s cold light,
Absorb myself in ventures, day and night.
A pyramid of stuff, a modern crop,
Where dreams take cruise, and fortunes never stop.

The visual whispers, "motion is the key,"
But I speak of inertia, haunting me.
A ripple starts, a tremor in the soul,
A festival of faces takes its toll.

I watch them drift, antennae raised so high,
Seeking connection in the digital sky.
The pyramid crumbles, grain by fading grain,
A shallow promise, whispered in the rain.

This digital cruise, a siren’s gentle call,
Offering answers, hiding shadows all.
My venture stalls, its future takes no hold,
A shallow story, rarely to be told.
The ripples grow, the motion now will cease,
I’ll absorb myself in finding inner peace.
